# Environment defaults

`setup.sh` reads its config from the **shell environment** — it does not source a
`.env`, and it aborts if a required var is unset. Config and credentials are kept
in **two** files
| File | Contents | Committed? |
|---|---|---|
| [`../vss.config.env`](../vss.config.env) | non-secret config (models, ports, tuning) | yes |
| `../vss.secrets.env` | credentials, generated by [`../scripts/gen-secrets.sh`](../scripts/gen-secrets.sh) | **no — gitignored** |

## How to apply

Generate the secrets file once, then source both in the same shell before
`setup.sh`:

```bash
./.github/skills/vss-up/scripts/gen-secrets.sh        # creates vss.secrets.env if absent
source .github/skills/vss-up/vss.config.env
source .github/skills/vss-up/vss.secrets.env
source setup.sh --summary     # or --search / --summary --search / --summary-and-search
```

Both files use plain `export` lines, so sourcing them **wins** over whatever is
in your shell. To keep an existing shell value instead, comment that line out.
Edit `vss.config.env` directly to change a non-secret default.

`gen-secrets.sh` writes **strong random** credentials, created once and reused on
later runs so Postgres/MinIO/RabbitMQ data volumes stay valid. To use your own
credentials (vault/CI), export them before running the script — it reuses any
credential already set rather than randomizing it. Rotate with
`gen-secrets.sh --force` (note: this invalidates existing data volumes).

## Which vars matter per mode

| Mode | Beyond the common creds, also needs |
|---|---|
| `--summary` | `VLM_MODEL_NAME`, `ENABLED_WHISPER_MODELS`, `OD_MODEL_NAME` |
| `--search` | `MULTIMODAL_EMBEDDING_MODEL` (+ `EMBEDDING_PROCESSING_MODE`) |
| `--dual` | superset of summary + search |
| `--unified` | summary vars + `TEXT_EMBEDDING_MODEL` |

Common to every mode: `MINIO_ROOT_USER/PASSWORD`, `POSTGRES_USER/PASSWORD`,
`RABBITMQ_USER/PASSWORD`. `EMBEDDING_PROCESSING_MODE` must be `sdk` or `api`.

## Caveats

- **GPU**: use a GPU VLM (`OpenVINO/Phi-3.5-vision-instruct-int8-ov`) and set
  `VLM_TARGET_DEVICE=GPU` — both noted inline in `vss.config.env`.
- **OVMS LLM summary**: if `ENABLE_OVMS_LLM_SUMMARY[_GPU]=true`, also set
  `OVMS_LLM_MODEL_NAME`.
- **Gated models**: a HuggingFace token is a secret — export `HUGGINGFACE_TOKEN`
  in your shell (or add it to `vss.secrets.env`) and set `GATED_MODEL=true`;
  never hardcode it in `vss.config.env`.
- **Production**: the generated credentials are dev-grade. On shared/production
  hosts, inject managed secrets (export them before `gen-secrets.sh`, or supply
  `vss.secrets.env` from your secret store).
